description:
First atomic step of the typed closure ABI epic. Drops the codegen-side
force that makes every closure_target_fns spec emit with all-Tagged
param_reprs / return_repr.

The force lives at src/ir_codegen.rs:2139-2143 (param_reprs) and
~2161 (return_reprs). It rewrites every param/return in a
closure-target spec to ArgRepr::Tagged. The typer already produces
narrow specs with typed Descrs; the force is the only thing keeping
those typed reprs from being honored.

## Acceptance (via golden CLIF diffs)

Run the existing test suite. Run the golden_clif test. Expected:

- Some goldens diff. The diffs should show closure-target fn bodies
  using their typed reprs (RawInt args / returns where the typer
  narrowed). The MakeClosure sites still store Tagged-encoded
  captures (that's 22.2's job). The CallClosure sites still pass
  Tagged args (22.3's job).

- Therefore: this step alone may break correctness for closure-using
  fixtures, because producer (caller) and consumer (closure body) no
  longer agree on the seam repr. That is EXPECTED. 22.2 + 22.3 land
  the matching caller-side changes.

- The .clif files BLESS-rewrite cleanly under BLESS=1, so we can see
  exactly which closure-target signatures changed and how.

- For NON-closure fixtures (add1.fz, tail_recursion.fz — no
  MakeClosure), the goldens should be unchanged.

If correctness breaks for ALL fixtures that use closures, that is a
green light to proceed with 22.2 — the typed body sigs are exposed,
now we wire the caller side. If non-closure goldens change, that's a
signal that closure_target_fns is leaking into fns that should be
typed-direct only, and we have a bug to investigate first.

## Out of scope

- Caller-side changes (22.2 / 22.3 / 22.4).
- Cont seam narrowing (22.4).
- Halt-cont typing (22.5).

## How to ship

Single commit. Run BLESS=1 cargo test golden_clif to record the
post-force-drop goldens. Note in the commit message that closure-using
fixtures may have broken correctness pending 22.2+. The intermediate
state is intentional and the goldens make it auditable.

If the user prefers "no intermediate-broken-correctness commit," we
land 22.1 through 22.4 together as one big commit. Smaller commits
are cleaner if reviewers can read the per-step golden diffs.
title: fz-cps-closure.1: drop closure_target_fns all-Tagged force
